name: Beluga Sturgeon
conservationEfforts: CITES regulates international caviar trade. Aquaculture reduces pressure on wild stocks. Caspian Sea countries coordinate conservation. Restocking programs release juveniles.
description: The largest freshwater fish in the world by weight, historically reaching 7 meters and 1,500 kg. Prized for its caviar (beluga caviar can cost $10,000/kg), it has been fished to near extinction.
